channel.title: Devops on Sergio Méndez Website
channel.description: Recent content in Devops on Sergio Méndez Website

item.title: Dev versus Ops in Kubernetes - Open Mic in Discord, Cloud Native Guatemala, Online
item.description:
In this session, we’ll have open mics on our Discord server this Thursday, February 26th, from 8:00 to 9:30 PM, so we can share our experiences working on Kubernetes deployments and how we communicate with developers who need their applications deployed. We’ll explore the following questions:

  • Was the application failing due to your error or the developer’s? How did you resolve it?
  • Did an upgrade break the application?
  • My ideal deployment was…
  • My worst deployment was…
  • What could I have done better in that deployment?
  • I don’t understand how this Kubernetes option works.

I hosted and spoke in this Podcast in Discord.

Event | Spotify

item.title: Presenter, ChatLoopBackOff Episode 75: Exploring Trulens with Shivay Lamba, CNCF, Online
item.description:
In this I presented Shivay in the CNCF show “ChatLoopBackOff Episode 75: Exploring Trulens with Shivay Lamba”

I participated as presenter in this CNCF Show.

Event

item.title: AI, Cloud Native and Kubernetes, Cloud Native Guatemala, Online
item.description:
In this online talk, Sergio Méndez, Edgar Alvarez, and José Lorenzana will discuss how cloud-native technologies like Kubernetes are being used for artificial intelligence applications. During the talk, we’ll explore the CNCF landscape in the field of artificial intelligence.

I hosted and participated as presenter. Event

item.title: Harbor at KubeCon Salt Lake City Interview by Sergio Méndez, Youtube, SLC Utah
item.description: Harbor KubeCon Salt Lake City Interview by Sergio Méndez recorded at KubeCon NA Utah 2024.

item.title: The role of CNCF Ambassadors, Twitter, X, Online
item.description:
I shared in this X Space my own experience to become a CNCF Ambassador.

X Space | Twitter

item.title: Ecosistema Cloud Native, Cloud Native Guatemala, UMG University, Cobán, Guatemala
item.description:
I spoke about the CNCF ecosystem, but this time for an university outside the city in Cobán Alta Verapaz.

Event | LinkedIn

item.title: Corre WebAssemply en edge usando WasmEdge, KCD Argentina, Argentina
item.description: I spoke about how to run WebAssembly on the edge using WasmEdge.

item.title: Preparando Submarinos en el Edge con Kubernetes, KCD Argentina, Online
item.description: I spoke about how to automate the process of create an argentinian hot drink called submarine, using edge computing and cloud native technologies like Kubernetes.

item.title: Cloud Native for Humans, KCD Islamabad, Pakistan Islambad, Online
item.description: I spoke about basic concepts around Kubernetes, the cloud native ecosystem and different groups were you can contribute.

item.title: Earth Day!, New York Kubernetes Meetup, Online
item.description:
I particiapted in the Earth Day themed event from New York Kubernetes Meetup, sharing about the CNCF Environmental Sustainability Technical Advisory Group (TAG ENV).

Event

item.title: Grafana and data visualization dashboards, USAC, Guatemala, Online
item.description: I spoke about the basic of how to use Grafana to visualize data in real-time, this session was shared to the Operating Systems course in USAC university.

item.title: Kube Dev Day, Cloud Native Guatemala, Agexport, Guatemala
item.description:
Hosted the event and presented speakers in the event.

Event | LinkedIn

item.title: Women in Cloud Native GT, Cloud Native Guatemala, Agexport, Guatemala
item.description:
I hosted the meetup dedicted to females, they shared about how they are using Kubernetes. I also found and ex professor and student in the event, it was awesome to see 3 generations in one session.

Event | LinkedIn

item.title: Despliegues en Edge con Flux, Cloud Native Guatemala
item.description:
I spoke at Cloud Guatemala with the topic “Edge deployments with Flux” it was about how to take advantage of Flux at the edge to update devices. I also hosted the event with other organizers.

Event | LinkedIn Post | Photo 1

item.title: Cooking a Sustainable Feijoada with Edge Cloud Native Technologies, KCD Sao Paulo, Brasil
item.description:
I spoke at Cloud Guatemala with the topic “Edge deployments with Flux” it was about how to take advantage of Flux at the edge to update devices. I also hosted the event with other organizers.

Event | LinkedIn Post | Photos

item.title: Cloud Native para Humanos, Tech Community, Guatemala
item.description:
I participated in the Tech Community Days, giving a quick introduction to CNCF and Kubernetes using drawings. I also covered topics as Service Meshes, certifications, env sustainability and also how to start contributing to CNCF projects.

Video 1 | Banner | Photo 1 | Photo 2

item.title: Intro: Ecosistema CNCF, Cloud Native Guatemala, Online
item.description:
I was host in the speaking session “Intro: Ecosistema CNCF”, I introduced the speaker and helped reading the questions in the session. Also in charge of the streaming. The topic was an introduction about CNCF.

Event

item.title: Buy vs. Build in DevOps, Uptime community,virtual
item.description:
Join us for a lively discussion on the age-old debate of “Buy vs. Build in DevOps” with five industry experts. Our panelists will explore the benefits and drawbacks of purchasing pre-built software versus building custom solutions, and share their insights on when each approach is the best choice. Don’t miss out on this insightful online event!

Video & Event

item.title: Students non code contributions for Linkerd and other CNCF projects, Sergio & Flynn, Representing CNCF,CCOSS, México
item.description:
In this talk we presented with Flynn from Buoyant and Linkerd community how students can contribute with and without code to the CNCF projects, in special how students are contributing to Linkerd.

Event Link | Video | Slides

item.title: Driving innovation with Keptn and Interoperability, Brad McCoy, Sergio Mendez, Keptn Community Day, in person, Detroit
item.description:
In this talk Sergio explained the theory around Keptn and Brad show a practical demonstration about how to use Keptn with Testkube.

Event Link | Photo | Slides
